ops.text:
## E19.B — contested-panel validation of c003-m2 vs baseline v6 — PRE-REGISTRATION (before any spend)

Authorized by team-lead as the trimmed Option D (deferred from E19, ledger 946f32cf). VALIDATION-ONLY: no new GEPA mutation rounds. Driver: opus-4.8 teammate. Run from jj workspace /Users/yasyf/Code/cc-steer-prep under `eval "$(ccp env)"` + `uv run --no-sync`. Never writes the live ~/.cc-steer/feedback.db — every db is a fresh copy-on-write clone (E8 discipline). No repo/jj/git mutations, no deployment.

### Why
E19 closed `below_mde` because baseline v6 sits at 0.946 combined on a golden-heavy panel (headroom 0.054 < MDE 0.108) — an instrument-power ceiling, not evidence c003-m2 is no better. The real signal lives in the E18-contested stratum (v6 = 0.8125 there). E19.B builds a large CLEAN contested panel and asks, with power, whether c003-m2's best-of-loop prompt actually beats v6 on exactly the rows E18 flags v6 unreliable.

### Budget: $80 HARD cap
Phase 1 mint ~$41 (109 rows @ opus, ~$0.372/row) + Phase 2 score ~$21 (2 arms × ~125 rows @ sonnet, ~$0.084/row) + conditional Phase 3 opus spot-check ~$15-20. Tracked via the WFA capture shim (sum total_cost_usd envelopes); hard-stop before $80. If the conditional opus spot-check would push over $80, STOP and check back rather than exceed.

### Phase 1 — MINT REFERENCES (durable infra)
Target = the ~109 E18 top_contested dedup_keys that are in the inputs db, not golden, and have NO existing auditor reference (the v6-only rows Option B dropped). Mint via the SAME audit-stage path that produced the existing 16 auditor refs: on a fresh clone, `run_verdicts(rows, prompt_builder(AUDIT_PROMPT, fid), structured_judge(Verdict, tier="large"), persist_verdict(store, role=AUDITOR, prompt_version=AUDIT_VERSION=5, model=resolved_model("large")=opus))` — auditor is blind (prompt from the event row only, never sees the judge verdict), one pass, opus. Existing 16 + newly-minted ~109 = the ~125-row CLEAN contested panel. Write it as a durable reusable file `E19/out/contested-panel.json` with per-row provenance: dedup_key, source_kind, text, reference(accepted), reference_source(auditor), category, model(opus), audit_version(5), mint=fresh|existing, plus E18 metadata (majority_category, p_contested, sigma_eb).

### Phase 2 — SCORE (paired, contested-stratum)
Arms: c000-baseline (live v6 JUDGE_PROMPT) vs c003-m2 (best-of-E19 evolved prompt) ONLY. Both re-vote the FULL ~125-row contested panel at --tier medium (sonnet, same tier as the E19 screen), fresh clone per arm, concurrency 8. Reward per row = 1 if arm's accept-decision matches the auditor reference, else 0. Metric = CONTESTED-STRATUM ACCURACY over all ~125 rows (no golden dilution). Paired per-row (same panel both arms).

### MDE (pre-registered, paired design at actual n≈125)
Card formula, paired: MDE_paired = 2.8016 · sqrt(p(1−p)/n), card p=0.0583, n=125 → SE=0.02094, **MDE_paired ≈ 0.059**. (The card's published two-sample form carries a sqrt(2) two-independent-samples factor; the paired design compares both arms on the SAME rows, so that factor drops — 0.059 is the card formula at the paired design. Two-sample reference value at n=125 = 0.083.) Card p=0.0583 is the instrument's pre-specified per-row error proxy; the contested panel's higher base error is captured by the DATA-DRIVEN paired-bootstrap CI, which is the binding significance gate.

### DECISION RULE (pre-registered)
c003-m2 VALIDATES iff BOTH: (a) paired-bootstrap 95% CI on mean per-row difference (c003-m2 − baseline) EXCLUDES zero (lo > 0), AND (b) delta ≥ MDE_paired 0.059. Also report McNemar (discordant b,c + exact p). 
- If validated at sonnet → run ONE opus disagreement-row spot-check (only rows where the two prompts' verdicts differ, both arms, at opus) before calling it, because the DEPLOYED judge is opus and tier-transfer must be spot-verified. Budget-gate this at $80.
- If CI includes zero OR delta < MDE → E19 closes as a REAL NULL; the held prompt-cache patch ships WITHOUT a prompt change (no PROMPT_VERSION prompt bump).

### Context
This validation gates the held prompt-cache ship: a validated c003-m2 ships WITH the cache restructure in one PROMPT_VERSION bump; a null ships the cache restructure alone. Single opus-auditor reference per contested row is the pre-registered reference standard (same as the existing 16) — a known limitation on genuinely-contested rows, mitigated by n≈125 and the paired design.

Next: extract the exact 109 mint keys, run Phase 1 mint on a fresh clone, build contested-panel.json.
